message:
🐛 Use CSS transform scaling for tiles to preserve content (#144)

Instead of calling fitAddon.fit() which resizes xterm.js internally
(causing alternate screen buffer content to vanish), tile mode now
uses CSS transform: scale() to visually fit terminals into tiles.

The terminal stays at its original size — no internal resize, no PTY
resize — so content from Claude Code and other agent CLIs is preserved.

When exiting tile mode, the CSS scale is removed and fitAddon.fit()
restores the terminal to full single-mode size.

When a tile is focused (clicked), a PTY resize is sent so the user
can interact with the correctly-sized terminal.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
